description: 171 - 04-04-2025 - Emails between Peter Mandelson, Ailsa Terry (Private Secretary for Foreign Affairs, No.10) and Varun Chandra (Prime Minister's Special Adviser on
text:
Foreign Affairs, No.10) and Varun Chandra (Prime Minister's Special Adviser on

Business and Investment, No.10)

Subject: RE: Next steps further

Date: Friday, 4 April 2025 at 21:43:41 British Summer Time
From: Ailsa Terry

To: Peter Mandelson, Morgan McSweeney, Varun Chandra

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Peter

Thank you for this sound advice and for the call earlier. Let's keep in close touch next
week when the dust has settled a little.

As discussed, | have created a named list of individuals on the trade talks issue and
beyond that we will be exerting maximum discipline and being very careful to avoid any
running commentary in the media.

Ailsa

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

From: Peter Mandelson Personal

Sent: 04 April 2025 16:20

To: Morgan McSweeney Personal ; Varun Chandra
Personal ; Ailsa Terry Personal

Subject: Next steps further

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

| haven't seen full read out of the call this morning but it’s pretty clear me

490

 In the meantime, we
will be undertaking our own assessment of the implications for the UK economy
and the consultation with business and the call for evidence has already started
The important point for Britain is to do everything possible
The immediate need is to control the information ﬂow out of Whitehall. We must
get our version of events out before others do, including those who have always
been sceptical about the possibility of a deal.
Peter
Lord Mandelson
HM Ambassador, Washington DC
Follow us online: www.gov.uk/fcdo
This email is intended for the addressee(s) only: All messages sent and received by the Foreign,
Commonwealth & Development Oﬃce may be monitored in line with relevant UK legisla]on
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
***
***
Personal
491
